Find a Job Apprenticeship

PLEASE BE ADVISED:

 All Department of Workforce Solutions, America’s Job Center New Mexico, and New Mexico Works offices will be closed on Friday, May 29, 2026, due to technology upgrade and transition. Websites and online systems including the Unemployment Insurance Tax & Claims system will also be temporarily unavailable during the transition process. 

ATTENTION SPIRIT AIRLINE EMPLOYEES: Rapid Response Services for Displaced Spirit Airlines Employees | U.S. Department of Labor

IMPORTANT - IF YOU HAVE AN OVERPAYMENT of a CARES Act/Pandemic related unemployment program benefit, click Here for more information.

Let us help you find what you're looking for.

Popular topics include: unemployment benefits, apprenticeship, jobs
  • IMPORTANT - Information about CARES Act/Pandemic overpayment You may qualify for a waiver of your CARES Act/Pandemic unemployment benefit overpayment. Click here to learn more.
  • Federal Government Shutdown Federal employees in New Mexico who are furloughed or laid off during a government shutdown may apply for Unemployment Insurance starting the first day, the New Mexico Department of Workforce Solutions (NMDWS) urges those employees to prepare by gathering necessary documents in advance.
  • Bank On New Mexico Bank On New Mexico works to ensure that everyone has access to a safe, affordable bank or credit union account. Click here to learn more.
  • Job loss due to natural disaster? If your employment was lost or interrupted due to a natural disaster you may be eligible for Unemployment Insurance benefits. To file a standard Unemployment Insurance Claim, apply online at https://www.jobs.dws.nm.gov, by phone at 1-877-664-6984 Monday-Friday, 8:00am-4:30pm, or at any America’s Job Center New Mexico location. To find an office near you visit https://www.dws.nm.gov/Office-Locations.
  • America’s Job Center New Mexico Workforce Connection is now America's Job Center New Mexico! Stay tuned as we roll out the new brand over the coming months! Click the image to learn more.
  • Jobs in the Clean Energy Workforce Click the image to read the full Executive Order 2024-152. Building New Mexico's Workforce to Foster the Clean Energy Transition, Infrastructure Investment and Resilient Communities.
  • Labor Relations Division Annual Report FY24 The Labor Relations Division Annual Report highlights the statutory mandates for the investigation, compliance, and support services for the Division’s five distinct units: Public Works, Wage & Hour, Paid Leave, Child Labor Compliance, and Human Rights.
  • Good Job New Mexico! The official podcast for the New Mexico Department of Workforce Solutions. Dive into engaging conversations, insightful interviews, and thought-provoking discussions around the work journeys from New Mexicans in all different industries and backgrounds.
  • 1st National Youth Apprenticeship Week Click the image to learn more.
  • Report Identity Theft If you suspect that your personal information has been stolen or if you are a business that falsely received UI correspondence report it to NMDWS.
  • Heroes Hiring Heroes Heroes Hiring Heroes job fair taking place in 5 cities during the month of April. Events are open to the public with a focus on military veterans, transitioning service members, and miliary families. Click the photo to learn more and find an event near you.
  • 12th Annual National Apprenticeship Week National Apprenticeship Week recognizes leaders in business, labor, education, and trade industry partners, and highlights how apprenticeships prepare workers for the jobs in demand today and in the future.
  • Employer Webinar Series NMDWS Employer Webinar Series is designed to keep employers informed and equipped to thrive in today’s dynamic workforce landscape. View the current webinar “Thrive: NMDWS Helping Your Business Evolve in a Changing Labor Market” and much more at www.YouTube.com/NMDWS Click the photo to view the full playlist
  • Recruitment Events Several robust recruitment events will take place on February 28 and March 7 at America’s Job Center New Mexico locations across the state. Attendees will have a chance to connect with employers ready to hire including New Mexico State agencies, private employers, and local municipalities.

We Educate, Empower, Employ, and Enforce.

The New Mexico Department of Workforce Solutions strives to be a leader in improving employment and poverty rates through workforce development, enhanced services for employers, and ensuring fair labor practices and workforce protections for New Mexicans.

Are you a Job Seeker?

Access career resources and services. Apply or certify for Unemployment Insurance benefits.

Learn More

Are you a Worker?

Find information about your legal rights as a worker, minimum wage, working as a teen, discrimination, and more.

Learn More

Are you a Business?

Access recruitment assistance and services to find the perfect candidate. Find information on Unemployment Insurance Tax, minimum wage, and more.

Learn More

Are you a Researcher?

Your source for economic and workforce data and analysis to help you make informed decisions.

Learn More

Wage and Hour Information

The Labor Relations Division enforces payment of wages, minimum wage, and overtime labor laws under the Wage Pay Act and the Minimum Wage Act, and also enforces the payment of prevailing wages to laborers and mechanics under the Public Works Minimum Wage Act.

What is the minimum wage?
New Mexico's current minimum wage is $12.00 per hour. Certain cities require a higher minimum wage.

Learn More

I have questions about my wages or work hours.
Review our frequently asked questions about Wage & Hour rights.

Learn More

I haven’t been paid what I’ve earned. What can I do?
You can file a wage claim (a request to attempt to recover unpaid wages or other compensation).

Learn More

What are the wages for public works projects?
NMDWS sets the minimum wage rates for all Public Works projects each year.

Learn More

Unemployment Information

Unemployment Insurance provides temporary financial assistance to qualified individuals who meet state eligibility requirements. Unemployment Insurance benefits are financed through employer payroll taxes; they are not deducted from employee paychecks.

I just lost my job. Now what?

You may qualify for temporary financial assistance while you look for work.

Learn More

How do I apply for unemployment benefits?
You can apply online at www.jobs.dws.nm.gov or over the phone at 1-877-664-6984 Monday-Friday, 8:00am-4:30pm.
Where do I file my weekly certification for unemployment benefits?
Complete your weekly certification online at www.jobs.dws.nm.gov or over the phone at 1-877-664-6984 Monday-Friday, 8:00am-4:30pm.

I want to find a job. Can you help?
Search for work online at www.jobs.dws.nm.gov or visit a America's Job Center New Mexico near you for personal assistance.
  • Do you want to become an

    Applications Developer?

    Develop, create, and modify general computer applications software or specialized utility programs. Analyze user needs and develop software solutions. Design software or customize software for client use with the aim of optimizing operational efficiency.

  • Do you want to become a

    Plumber?

    Assemble, install, alter, and repair pipelines or pipe systems that carry water, steam, air, or other liquids or gases. May install heating and cooling equipment and mechanical control systems.

  • Do you want to become an

    Ironworker?

    Raise, place, and unite iron or steel girders, columns, and other structural members to form completed structures or structural frameworks. May erect metal storage tanks and assemble prefabricated metal buildings.

  • Do you want to become an

    Electrician?

    Perform preliminary work with the material and tools of the trade; residential and commercial rough wiring and finish work including the installation of various kinds of wires, cables, and conduits per the National Electrical Code; industrial lighting and service installation; motors controls and installation including emergency generators, relays, and timing devices.

Can't find what you're looking for?

Contact us and we'll do our best to answer your questions.



PHYSICAL ADDRESS
401 Broadway NE, Albuquerque, NM 87102

MAILING ADDRESS
PO Box 1928, Albuquerque, NM 87103
